models.strategies: A configured trading strategy with entry/exit rules, assigned instruments, position sizing, and evaluation schedule. Strategies can be Trend Following, Mean Reversion, or Composite (master entry with multiple exit strategies).
models.backtests: A record of running a strategy against historical market data. Includes the configuration used (date range, capital, execution model) and summary metrics when completed.
models.backtestDetail: The full results of a completed backtest: performance metrics, equity curve chart data, individual trades, benchmark comparisons, monthly returns, and any anti-pattern warnings detected.
models.walkForwardOptimizations: A walk-forward optimization run that tests strategy parameters across rolling train/validation windows to prevent overfitting.
models.walkForwardDetail: The full results of a walk-forward optimization: window-by-window results with best parameters and validation metrics.
models.comparisonData: A side-by-side comparison of multiple backtest results, including ranked metrics and normalized equity curves.

relationships: Each Strategy has many Backtests (historical test runs)
Each Strategy has many Walk-Forward Optimizations
Each Backtest belongs to a Strategy and contains a BacktestDetail when completed
Each Walk-Forward Optimization belongs to a Strategy and contains many Windows
Comparison Data references multiple Backtests from different Strategies
Strategies reference Instruments by symbol (AAPL, MSFT, etc.)

strategies.name: Trend Following - Daily
strategies.description: Classic dual moving average crossover strategy with RSI filter. Enters on golden cross, exits on death cross or trailing stop.

parameters.name: activation_pct
parameters.description: Profit needed to move stop to break-even

strategies.name: Mean Reversion - Hourly
strategies.description: Bollinger Band mean reversion with RSI confirmation. Buys at lower band when oversold, sells at upper band when overbought.

strategies.name: Momentum Breakout
strategies.description: Breakout strategy using 52-week highs with volume confirmation. Enters when price breaks above resistance on above-average volume.

parameters.name: volume_multiplier
parameters.description: Volume must exceed N times average

strategies.name: Pairs Trading - Tech
strategies.description: Statistical arbitrage between correlated tech stocks. Trades the spread when it deviates from historical mean by more than 2 standard deviations.

strategies.name: Volatility Squeeze
strategies.description: Composite strategy: enters on Bollinger Band squeeze (low volatility breakout) with Keltner Channel confirmation. Master entry with 4 managed exit strategies.

strategies.name: RSI Divergence
strategies.description: Detects bullish and bearish RSI divergences against price action. Enters when RSI diverges from price trend, suggesting a reversal.

parameters.name: divergence_lookback
parameters.description: Bars to look back for divergence

backtests.errorMessage: Insufficient data for GOOGL: only 180 trading days available, minimum 252 required

backtestDetail.recommendationText: Strategy meets all production thresholds. Sharpe 1.45 exceeds minimum 1.0. Max drawdown -18.2% within -25% limit. Walk-forward validation recommended before deployment.

antiPatternWarnings.message: 142 total trades over 5 years (28/year). Consider testing on additional instruments to increase sample size for more statistically significant results.
antiPatternWarnings.recommendation: Add more instruments or use shorter timeframe to increase trade count above 200.

strategyTypes.label: Trend Following
strategyTypes.description: Follow market momentum using moving average crossovers and trend indicators. Best for sustained directional moves.

strategyTypes.label: Mean Reversion
strategyTypes.description: Trade against extreme price moves using Bollinger Bands and RSI. Buys oversold, sells overbought conditions.

strategyTypes.label: Composite
strategyTypes.description: Master-slave pattern combining an entry strategy with multiple managed exit strategies (stop-loss, trailing, break-even).
